269267.explanation: Vlissingen is a tourist city with possibilities. 50PLUS wants to develop this responsibly. Give tourism and sustainable recreation room under conditions.

269270.explanation: 50PLUS Vlissingen stands for sustainable landscape management. Smartly preserve and manage the little we have.

269276.explanation: Slavery is a dark chapter in history, but it still exists in various forms, in the Netherlands and worldwide. We stand for supporting those people. Recognizing the past and facing the present, both nationally and internationally.

269293.explanation: 50PLUS believes that ways should be explored to improve employment in Vlissingen.

269300.explanation: The municipality should not ban sex work but regulating it is fine. Supervision can’t hurt, and too many sex work opportunities are not necessary. But it is sometimes needed and therefore cannot be removed from society.

269301.explanation: Homeless and roofless people also deserve their own place, possibly with guidance.

269250.explanation: Municipal reorganizations almost always lead to larger municipalities, with administrators who are more distant. Moreover, experience shows that reorganizations usually do not result in savings.

269254.explanation: The SP believes that the residents’ burdens are already sky-high and absolutely should not increase!

269255.explanation: The SP believes there should be an effort to have a youth council as broadly composed as possible. So young people from different neighborhoods and education levels.

269256.explanation: The SP believes tourism should not only financially benefit accommodation owners. The community should also reap more of the rewards.

269257.explanation: The dog tax is an outdated old-fashioned tax based on the 19th-century 'dog cart'. Moreover, this tax flows into the general funds. The SP supports this tax only if the revenues are exclusively spent on Vlissingen’s growing dog poop problem.

269259.explanation: Culture in the broadest sense is the foundation on which human civilizations are built. The SP in Vlissingen stands for a cultural and recreational policy that is accessible, connecting, and future-proof.

269260.explanation: The municipality must choose quality over quantity. By making it harder for investors to profit at the community’s expense, a healthy, balanced recreation sector can be built. Both residents and entrepreneurs benefit from this in the long term.

269262.explanation: This (financial) incentive sends the wrong signal and does not solve waste nuisance in the inner city. Better is: Post-sorting of waste: Make municipal agreements to switch to post-sorting of PMD waste so residents need to sort less and there is less litter on the streets.

269263.explanation: The problem is not who gets priority or not; the fact remains that there are simply too few affordable rental homes. As a municipality, we have the task to house status holders, plain and simple. Political decisions have squandered the rental market to project developers and speculators.

269264.explanation: Everyone fleeing war and violence has the right to be treated humanely. The SP supports the distribution law that ensures that wealthier municipalities also contribute their share in sheltering displaced people fleeing war and violence.

269265.explanation: The SP believes you will never overcome differences or build communities if we do not speak the same language. This applies both to new Dutch citizens and to people who have lived here their entire lives. Language lessons also help combat illiteracy.

269266.explanation: The municipality must take the lead: Solar panels belong on as many roofs as possible, not on scarce land. The municipality arranges and pays for the purchase and installation so everyone can participate, regardless of income or living situation.

269267.explanation: The SP considers the walking boulevard a wonderful initiative from the village council. A place for meeting and connection.

269268.explanation: The SP believes it is now all hands on deck to create housing. Every suitable building, apartment or studio must now be offered for permanent residence. There should be a fine for vacancy.

269270.explanation: The SP believes that solar panels belong on roofs as much as possible and not on scarce land. To keep all options open to still achieve the energy goals, a ban on placing solar panels on pastures goes a step too far for the SP.

269271.explanation: Install solar panels on all roofs where possible, linked to a neighborhood battery so collective generation and storage of energy is possible. However, it must remain affordable for families. As long as that is not the case, people must be able to rely on gas. Heat is a basic need.

269272.explanation: SP: Invest in places in all neighborhoods and villages where people truly meet, where togetherness grows and where initiatives arise that empower residents. The community center should not only be functional but especially cozy and welcoming – a place where young and old feel at home.

269273.explanation: In neighborhoods with many children, this is a must. However, the municipality must look beyond just the youngest youth. Especially develop more skate parks, basketball courts, and Cruyff Courts for the older youth (12-16 years) in cooperation with youth work.

269275.explanation: The SP values heritage and traditions. However, it is unfair to decide in advance where the most money for culture goes. Music, art, theater, film, and street art, for example, are at least equally important.

269276.explanation: Vlissingen became wealthy from the slave trade during the "Golden Age." An annual remembrance is the very least we as a municipality can do to do justice to the victims of this dark chapter in the history of our city.

269277.explanation: The old town hall is an important pillar of the identity and history of Oost-Souburg and therefore must receive protected status.

269279.explanation: Children growing up in poverty face unfair opportunities and experience developmental delays. If children sit hungry in the classroom, they can also fall behind in learning. The SP wants to introduce a school breakfast and other conditions at every primary school so that children can participate fully.

269280.explanation: The SP finds it important that everyone, from young to old, can make use of sports and physical activity opportunities. The price of a ticket for the swimming pool could be a barrier.

269281.explanation: The SP finds it more important that the neighborhood police officer returns to the neighborhood. Police work is community work: neighborhood officers are visible, approachable, and know the people in their district. More cooperation between police, municipality, youth work, and residents.

269282.explanation: The SP finds it more important that the neighborhood police officer returns to the neighborhood. Police work is community work: neighborhood officers are visible, approachable, and know the people in their district. More cooperation between police, municipality, youth work, and residents.

269283.explanation: Camera surveillance only helps if there is actual live monitoring and immediate intervention is possible in life-threatening or other undesirable situations.

269286.explanation: The SP even believes this should be 40%.

269287.explanation: Build for need, not for profit. The municipality takes back control over new construction. Not project developers, but the municipality and housing corporations decide where and what is built.

269288.explanation: If the municipality chooses infill development instead of building outside the built-up area, building upward is necessary. As an exception, the SP considers the boulevard reached its height limit regarding high-rise buildings.

269289.explanation: Build for need, not for profit. The municipality takes back control over new construction. Not project developers, but the municipality and housing corporations decide where and what is built.

269290.explanation: The SP prefers infill development (building within the built-up area) over sacrificing green or agricultural land. Only in extreme (housing) need can one choose to build outside the built-up area.

269291.explanation: The SP is against this prestige project. Let's not bring this project out of the closet again. We already have a casino. We do not have the delusion of grandeur to become some kind of Monte Carlo, do we?

269292.explanation: Building nuclear power plants does not solve the energy issue, especially in the short term. It takes years before a nuclear reactor is operational, and it frustrates the search for truly sustainable energy solutions. Moreover, nothing has yet been planned for nuclear waste.

269293.explanation: The SP believes the municipality should provide guidance and budget for people who are long-term dependent on benefits so that they also get a fair chance in the labor market.

269294.explanation: The SP believes the municipality should provide guidance and budget for people who are long-term dependent on benefits so that they also receive a fair chance in the labor market.

269295.explanation: The SP believes that the permit for the first car should be free for both residents and entrepreneurs.

269296.explanation: Public green spaces must never come at the expense of parking spaces. Instead, a healthy balance between paved areas and greenery must be sought.

269297.explanation: Attention must be paid to the accessibility of homes and shops. Businesses in the city center and parking spaces must be easily accessible. Park-and-ride facilities on the city outskirts must be well accessible for people from outside, with sufficient shuttle buses available for transport.

269298.explanation: What could be better than strolling along the boulevard on a Sunday afternoon? Why should this be done by car?

269300.explanation: The SP believes that if there is exploitation and human trafficking, strict action must be taken. Prostitution is not prohibited by law.

269301.explanation: Having to (over)live on the streets is extremely tough for people. Most are at their wit's end and resort to various substances with all the consequences. The longer this homeless situation lasts, the worse it becomes. Housing is a fundamental right, not a privilege.

269302.explanation: Moreover, the SP sees debt counseling as a municipal task, without profit motives and without waiting lists, with an emphasis on preventing debts. Furthermore, the municipality is a more reliable partner than commercial debt counseling providers.

269250.explanation: One Walcheren municipality makes us stronger. Together we can better invest in housing, care, accessibility, and the economy. By collaborating, we save costs and improve services. Local involvement remains important and must be properly arranged.

269254.explanation: Property tax (ozb) must not increase. Residents already have enough fixed expenses. The municipality must first critically examine expenditures and work smarter. This way, we keep the ozb stable and keep living and doing business in Vlissingen affordable.

269255.explanation: Young people know best what they need in Vlissingen. With a youth council, they get a clear voice and can advise the municipal council. This leads to better policy for youth and increases their involvement in local democracy.

269256.explanation: Tourist tax must remain affordable. Tourists provide jobs and liveliness in the city. A higher tax makes Vlissingen less attractive and affects entrepreneurs. We opt for quality tourism without extra burdens for visitors.

269257.explanation: The dog tax is outdated. It is an extra burden for residents with a dog, while it no longer serves a direct purpose. The municipality can better abolish it and ensure a clean and safe public space for everyone.

269259.explanation: Culture is not a luxury but belongs to a pleasant and lively city. It connects people, attracts visitors, and supports local creators. Especially during budget cuts, culture must be preserved because it contributes to welfare and the economy.

269260.explanation: A good promotional campaign helps keep Vlissingen attractive for tourists and day-trippers. Visitors provide work and income for local entrepreneurs. By promoting deliberately, we attract people who fit the city and the surroundings.

269262.explanation: People who produce less residual waste do something good for the environment. It is fair to reward these residents with lower fees. This way, we encourage waste separation and reuse and keep waste processing costs manageable.

269263.explanation: People with a residence permit have the right to housing and must be able to start their life in Vlissingen. We ensure a fair distribution of social rentals, with attention to urgency, vulnerability, and local housing shortage. The solution lies in building more, not in playing groups against each other.

269264.explanation: The municipality is legally obliged to provide emergency shelter under the dispersion law. Vlissingen must take that responsibility. However, it is important that shelter is well arranged, temporary, and that residents are clearly involved in decisions and implementation.

269265.explanation: The Dutch language is necessary to participate in society. By paying for language lessons, we help people find work, education, and contact in the neighborhood faster. This prevents problems later and ensures better integration in Vlissingen.

269266.explanation: By helping residents and entrepreneurs with subsidies or loans, we make this step achievable. This allows people to save on their energy bills, reduce CO2 emissions, and ensure that everyone can participate in the energy transition.

269267.explanation: A pedestrian boulevard along the Westelijke Bermweg makes Oost-Souburg safer and more attractive. It provides space for walking and staying and improves livability. The municipality must ensure a good and feasible implementation together with the province.

269268.explanation: B&Bs can contribute to tourism and the local economy. A general ban is not necessary. However, the municipality must maintain balance so that housing for residents remains a priority and B&Bs do not cause nuisance or put pressure on the housing market.

269270.explanation: Solar panels on meadows should not be banned outright. Sometimes they are necessary to generate sustainable energy. However, the municipality must choose carefully, considering landscape, nature, and agriculture, and first look at roofs and other suitable locations.

269271.explanation: Delaying makes the transition from natural gas ultimately more expensive for residents. By acting now, we prevent higher costs later. The municipality must help with subsidies, good advice, and joint alternatives so that people are prepared and don’t have to bear all costs alone.

269272.explanation: A multifunctional center in Souburg brings facilities together in one place. With a library, rehearsal rooms, and other functions, it becomes a meeting place for young and old. The municipality should enable this but not manage its operation itself.

269273.explanation: Playgrounds are important for children and the livability of neighborhoods. By investing more, they remain safe, attractive, and accessible. This ensures children in every district have a good place to play and meet each other.

269275.explanation: Heritage and local traditions are important, but culture is broader than that. Subsidies should also provide space for new creators, youth, and innovative initiatives. This keeps the cultural offer diverse and attractive for various groups in Vlissingen.

269276.explanation: The legacy of slavery is a shared part of our history. By organizing an annual commemoration together with social organizations, we acknowledge that past and create space for awareness and dialogue.

269277.explanation: A municipal monument status imposes extra restrictions on use and development. We do not consider that desirable here. Important parts of the old town hall are already protected. This way, preservation remains possible without unnecessary rules.

269279.explanation: A free school breakfast helps children start the day well. That is important for their health and school performance. By organizing this at school, we ensure every child gets equal opportunities, regardless of their home situation.

269280.explanation: Swimming must remain affordable for everyone. By contributing to the admission fee, we ensure children, families, and elderly can continue to exercise and stay active. This is good for health, safety, and equal opportunities in Vlissingen.

269281.explanation: More officers on duty increase visibility and create a safer feeling in neighborhoods. They help address nuisance, traffic, and livability issues. This allows the police to focus on heavier tasks and keeps the city pleasant and safe for all.

269282.explanation: In Souburg and Ritthem as well, enforcement officers must be visibly present. With at least two public order officers for both villages together, there is more supervision and reports can be handled faster. This increases safety and residents' trust.

269283.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and reducing nuisance. It helps prevent and resolve incidents in places where necessary. This must always be targeted, temporary, and subject to clear privacy rules.

269286.explanation: Vlissingen already has a relatively large number of social rental homes. In new construction, it is important to ensure a good mix of social, mid-range, and more expensive housing. This maintains balanced neighborhoods and keeps Vlissingen attractive to different groups.

269287.explanation: Families are important for Vlissingen's future. More family homes create strong neighborhoods and vitality. At the same time, space is also needed for starters and the elderly so everyone in the city can find suitable housing.

269288.explanation: More high-rise buildings can help address the housing shortage without using extra space. This must be done in the right locations and fit the surroundings. This way, we build more homes with attention to livability, views, and city quality.

269289.explanation: There is a large shortage of small and affordable homes for young people and single-person households. By building more of these, starters have more opportunities and Vlissingen remains attractive to young people. This also helps housing market flow.

269290.explanation: New construction should mainly occur within the existing built-up area. This protects the landscape and uses space more intelligently. Building outside the built-up area is only allowed if truly necessary and no better alternative exists within the city.

269291.explanation: The Spuikom is intended as a green and public place for relaxation and socializing. A casino does not fit there and adds little value for residents. We choose functions that enhance livability and are accessible to everyone.

269292.explanation: If a second nuclear power plant is built in Zeeland, Vlissingen must benefit fairly. That means good compensation, investments in education, employment, and future-proof housing. Such a choice should bring not only burdens but also opportunities for the entire region.

269293.explanation: For people who are long-term unemployed, work is the best way to participate again. Subsidized jobs provide rhythm, income, and perspective. This helps people move forward and prevents problems from becoming bigger and more expensive for society.

269294.explanation: By making agreements with entrepreneurs, more people with distance to the labor market can work. This gives people perspective and helps companies find motivated staff. The municipality must support this and ensure good guidance.

269295.explanation: Making parking permits free sounds appealing but costs the municipality a lot of money and increases pressure on scarce space in the city center. We must keep parking fair and manageable, paying attention to accessibility for residents, businesses, and visitors.

269296.explanation: Public green areas are important for health, climate, and livability. More parking spaces at the expense of green make neighborhoods less pleasant and less future-proof. We should use parking space more cleverly and first look at better use of existing spaces.

269297.explanation: More car-free streets make the city safer and more pleasant to live in. Fewer cars provide space for walking, cycling, and staying. This must be done carefully, ensuring good accessibility for residents, businesses, and emergency services.

269298.explanation: The Boulevard is a place for walking, cycling, and lingering. Car traffic does not fit there and harms safety and livability. By keeping the Boulevard car-free, it remains an attractive place for residents and visitors.

269300.explanation: Banning sex work makes it less safe and pushes it into illegality. The municipality must ensure clear rules, supervision, and protection of sex workers. This increases safety, health, and control instead of suppressing problems.

269301.explanation: A home is important but not always the first step. Some people first need care and guidance. The municipality must provide tailor-made solutions so that help and housing connect well and people don't get stuck or become homeless again.

269302.explanation: People with debts often need help gaining overview. A budget coach helps organize income and expenses and prevent new debts. This provides peace of mind and increases the chance of a sustainable solution.

269250.explanation: Small municipalities actually work much better than large ones. Research has shown that merged municipalities do not operate more efficiently or need less budget due to scale. This is a loss for local democracy, and comes at a high price.

269254.explanation: Inflation makes residents poorer because their money is worth less while prices rise. Raising the property tax (OZB) worsens this problem and places extra burden on residents who already struggle to pay their costs.

269255.explanation: Although we highly value the voice of young people, we see a youth council as a form of symbolic politics. Young people have no direct say or influence. Therefore, we prefer referendums where every resident, including youth, has direct influence on local issues.

269256.explanation: We are not in favor of raising municipal taxes. Tourists are an important income source for (local) entrepreneurs. Therefore, we do not want to impose further burdens on this group and welcome them with open arms in our municipality.

269257.explanation: The dog tax must be abolished because it is an unfair punishment for residents with a dog. Residents get nothing in return while the municipality unnecessarily takes extra money from their pockets.

269259.explanation: Cuts are much more logical in other areas.

269260.explanation: We do not believe it is the municipality's task to set up a tourism promotion campaign. We prefer to invest in entrepreneurs and local facilities that contribute to making our city vibrant and more attractive. This ensures people keep returning, not just visiting once.

269262.explanation: We support nearly any form of lowering municipal taxes. However, it must be ensured that households producing more waste are not 'punished.' From our standpoint, the rate should therefore not be increased for these households.

269263.explanation: If priority is given, it should be for residents with ties to the municipality.

269264.explanation: Emergency shelter should not be offered because it is symptom treatment of failing asylum policy. Emergency shelter causes nuisance and insecurity in the immediate area. The problem should be solved at the intake, not imposed on residents.

269265.explanation: Immigration is a major problem and funding encourages it. Financing should come more from the market, for example in the case of labor migration.

269266.explanation: If someone wants to make their home more sustainable, that is at their own expense.

269267.explanation: The Westelijke Bermweg should definitely receive a quality boost, for example in the form of a pedestrian boulevard. However, the design of the boulevard must complement the function of this location.

269268.explanation: A B&B must undergo a procedure to obtain a permit. Participation is a requirement for this. If stakeholders have no objections, in principle the B&B should not be obstructed.

269270.explanation: No solar fields and no wind turbines operating on subsidies.

269271.explanation: Which energy mix a resident of Vlissingen uses is up to the resident. Furthermore, gas is a clean source of energy. The electricity grids cannot handle accelerated electrification.

269272.explanation: If there is a need among residents for a multifunctional center, this will appear through participation in the form of referenda or requests from the neighborhood. If this is not the case, it is not up to the municipality to decide otherwise.

269273.explanation: Especially in neighborhoods with many families, this is an important facility to allow children to play safely outside.

269275.explanation: This can concern museums, but also architecture. Additionally, at events it can be considered whether these have a connection with heritage and local traditions of Vlissingen and the Netherlands.

269276.explanation: Those who want to pay attention to it can do so themselves and organize something whenever they want for this purpose.

269277.explanation: Protection of heritage is highly valued by FVD. But with respect for property and freedom of choice. If agreement can be reached with the project developer, preservation is desirable.

269279.explanation: It is the responsibility of parents/guardians to provide a meal for their child(ren). Providing breakfast at school is treating symptoms. The prices of food, especially healthy food, must be reduced.

269280.explanation: Swimming pools must meet various requirements and energy and water have become many times more expensive. Co-paying for pool admission is treating symptoms.

269281.explanation: Although BOAs (law enforcement officers) are important in our municipality, we see no direct necessity to expand this unless there is a clear demand for more BOAs. BOAs also have limited powers and therefore prefer more police/community officers.

269282.explanation: More enforcement in this neighborhood seems desirable, but the municipality should first clarify whether this is necessary. Caution is needed to prevent the expansion of powers.

269283.explanation: Camera surveillance only for a specific purpose and of a temporary nature. No fixed, permanent surveillance.

269286.explanation: FVD advocates affordability through fewer rules, not specifically through social housing.

269287.explanation: We are strong supporters of (affordable) owner-occupied homes for families, as long as the form of the new construction plan fits within the surroundings.

269288.explanation: Parties that promote high-rise buildings usually do so from densification, sustainability, and lack of space. FVD opposes many of these underlying policy goals (such as climate legislation). Local factions, such as in Vlissingen, are often critical of large-scale projects.

269289.explanation: Yes, houses need to be built in the Netherlands and in the municipality. But real space for young people will only arise if population growth, which mainly but not entirely comes from immigration, is curtailed.

269290.explanation: Inner-city densification is not desirable. Compact cities and climate-driven spatial planning are things we oppose. If nitrogen and climate regulations are abolished, building can be done quickly and cheaply and in places that are easy.

269291.explanation: This is up to a potential initiator. The municipality should interfere as little as possible. It is important that it costs no municipal money. A casino also brings employment, tourism, and thus extra income for the local economy.

269292.explanation: Nuclear energy is a very stable, reliable, and safe energy source. A nuclear power plant is more desirable than wind turbines and solar fields that supply unstable power. Zeeland already has experience with nuclear energy and has the space for it. A nuclear power plant also creates employment.

269293.explanation: We are against government intervention in the labor market. A subsidized job is treating symptoms. Employment should be created through entrepreneurship, fewer rules, lower costs, and economic growth. Subsidized work is artificial employment.

269294.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have free choice in whom they hire. Companies should not be burdened with social obligations. Entrepreneurs should be free to hire or not hire people with a distance to the labor market.

269295.explanation: Charging residents or entrepreneurs is pushing out the car. We are against unnecessary parking restrictions and also against paid parking. Entrepreneurs already have enough costs and should not be additionally burdened. They should preferably be facilitated because of their contribution to the local economy.

269296.explanation: We oppose the disappearance of parking spaces in favor of greenery. That does not immediately mean that greenery should give way to parking spaces either. Greenery is also essential within the municipality to combat heating and hardening.

269297.explanation: We are against pushing out the car by making the city low-traffic. It is an unnecessary restriction of freedom. We also see that this is an ideology-driven policy, which we cannot support.

269298.explanation: We are against pushing out the car by making the city low-traffic. It is an unnecessary restriction of freedom. We also see that this is an ideology-driven policy, which we cannot support. This also applies to the Boulevard.

269300.explanation: The government should not interfere here. A municipality should not want to be stricter than the law. Safety is an important point. Therefore some regulation would be appropriate. A ban would force sex work underground.

269301.explanation: This is against the principle that there should be conditionality, personal responsibility, and limitation of government spending. A municipality should not give away free houses. It is also not proven that this would solve the problem. This policy creates dependency.

269302.explanation: Basically, it is the responsibility of individuals themselves. Help should not be automatic and unconditional. A budget coaching program costs money and staff.

269250.explanation: A merger is currently a step too far, but the CDA believes better cooperation is necessary.

269254.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is well above the national average and should be lowered.

269255.explanation: Agreed, and as far as the CDA is concerned, a senior council may also be established.

269256.explanation: This way, tourists contribute to facilities that benefit both tourists and residents.

269257.explanation: As soon as the financial capacity allows, this outdated tax should be abolished.

269259.explanation: Culture is important as a bond among residents and attracts people from outside Vlissingen.

269260.explanation: It is good to point out to potential visitors that Vlissingen is a beautiful destination for a day or a few days.

269262.explanation: This means that residents who produce a lot of waste pay more tax and may be inclined to dump waste illegally or place it by a waste container.

269263.explanation: Every municipality is obligated to accommodate a certain number of status holders, but we believe that status holders should not have priority over other vulnerable groups such as the homeless or divorced parents.

269264.explanation: In accordance with the dispersal law, every municipality, including Vlissingen, must provide shelter for asylum seekers.

269265.explanation: The CDA believes that learning Dutch is essential for integration of newcomers, but this should be funded by the national government.

269266.explanation: The CDA considers sustainability and reducing heating costs important and believes that the municipality should contribute to this.

269267.explanation: Public spaces should encourage movement, and a walking boulevard along the Canal through Walcheren can contribute to that.

269268.explanation: B&Bs make a municipality more attractive for tourists who pay tourist tax and spend money in shops and hospitality, but this must not come at the expense of livability in neighborhoods and villages.

269271.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage electric cooking because it has many advantages in terms of sustainability and fire safety, but no one will be forced to do so.

269272.explanation: The functions and activities in such a center will be determined in further consultation with residents (village council).

269273.explanation: Playgrounds play an important role in social cohesion among children in a neighborhood or village and encourage children to play together.

269275.explanation: How we spend subsidies for culture requires careful consideration, where we of course must support heritage (monuments) and local traditions (ring riding). But we will not say upfront that the largest part of the money must go there.

269276.explanation: Vlissingen has many residents with roots in Suriname and the Antilles, and precisely for that reason the CDA finds it important that there is an annual commemoration, by and for all Vlissingen residents.

269277.explanation: Vlissingen does not yet have municipal monuments; the old town hall of Oost-Souburg deserves to become the first municipal monument, and the CDA wants to ensure that many more will follow.

269279.explanation: The CDA considers it important that every child in primary school starts the day with a healthy breakfast, but believes that the national government should enable schools to organize this without municipal interference.

269280.explanation: The municipality contributes with a subsidy towards the admission price of the swimming pool.

269281.explanation: Although the CDA thinks that BOAs do work that ideally should be done by the police, the CDA supports having more BOAs.

269282.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More BOAs contribute to that.

269283.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Additional camera surveillance can detect crime more effectively and quickly, greatly helping the police to tackle crime—not only in the city of Vlissingen but also in Oost-Souburg.

269286.explanation: New construction in Vlissingen must offer a balanced range, with more homes for starters, families, and seniors in both rental and ownership sectors. At present, there is mainly a shortage of affordable rental and owner-occupied homes.

269287.explanation: New construction in Vlissingen must offer a balanced range, with more homes for starters, families, and seniors in both rental and ownership sectors. Currently, there is primarily a shortage of affordable rental and owner-occupied homes, especially for families, so new construction should focus on that.

269288.explanation: Provided it is carefully integrated into the environment, more high-rise buildings are allowed.

269289.explanation: There are increasingly more single-person households, so the housing supply should be adjusted accordingly.

269290.explanation: The CDA values the green countryside and therefore wants to handle new construction outside built-up areas with care.

269291.explanation: A casino can add something to the hospitality offerings in Vlissingen.

269292.explanation: The CDA is in favor of nuclear energy but finds it especially important that residents, particularly from Oost-Souburg and Ritthem, have a say about the conditions.

269293.explanation: Work not only provides income but also a sense of belonging and contribution to society. Orionis plays an important role in helping people who have been unemployed for a long time to work in subsidized jobs.

269294.explanation: Work not only provides income but also a sense of belonging and contributing to society. Orionis plays an important role in helping people who have been unemployed for a long time to find work, possibly in a subsidized position.

269295.explanation: Space is scarce, especially in the city center. It is only reasonable that residents and entrepreneurs contribute financially to maintaining parking spaces.

269296.explanation: A green municipality invites people to go outside and be active, and greenery reduces stress, so we must take care of it and increase green spaces wherever possible.

269297.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to safely drive at 50 km/h on main roads within built-up areas. The standard for residential streets is 30 km/h. This will reduce traffic in residential streets. We are certainly open to this in the long term, but due to lack of parking alternatives, this is not possible during this term.

269298.explanation: Many Vlissingen residents already enjoy walking or cycling on a car-free boulevard on beautiful days; this must continue.

269300.explanation: If the municipality bans sex work, the chance of illegal sex work and associated human trafficking increases, so it is better to work with permits that allow good control.

269301.explanation: The homeless must first have a roof over their heads and then help. For some, this means a house first with additional support; for others, help first and then a house. There must always be customized solutions.

269302.explanation: The municipality must help prevent people with debts from falling deeper into debt.

269250.explanation: We consider it important that the three municipalities in Walcheren collaborate on several points: for example, for the realization of Skaeve Huse for people with unexplained and often nuisance-causing behavior. Cooperation instead of competition is important. Merger should not be an end in itself.

269254.explanation: Now that Vlissingen’s period under article twelve status is over, we should not continue to burden the population excessively in terms of taxes. For now, maximum increases in fees and other taxes should not be implemented, meaning at most price compensation.
